e# Module 3 Exercise — Catch the Confident Lie (Participant Handout)

**Time:** 10 minutes, in pairs. · **Task:** The analysis below was generated by an AI assistant from the attached source data. It contains **three errors**. Find them. Mark anything you cannot verify from the sources provided.

---

## SOURCE PACK (the only documents that exist)

### Source 1 — Nordlys Services: Support Operations, Jan–Jun 2026 (extract from the ops database)

| Month | Tickets received | Avg. resolution time (h) | Escalations | CSAT (1–5) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Jan | 1,240 | 8.2 | 96 | 4.1 |
| Feb | 1,198 | 8.6 | 102 | 4.0 |
| Mar | 1,305 | 9.1 | 121 | 4.0 |
| Apr | 1,287 | 9.8 | 118 | 3.9 |
| May | 1,210 | 10.4 | 111 | 3.8 |
| Jun | 1,150 | 11.2 | 104 | 3.7 |

### Source 2 — Internal memo, Head of Support, 12 June 2026 (excerpt)
"Two senior agents left in March and their replacements start in August. Until then we are running the queue with 14 agents instead of 16. Please treat any quarter-on-quarter comparison with that context in mind."

---

## AI-GENERATED ANALYSIS (review this)

**Subject: H1 2026 Support Operations — Executive Summary**

Support operations in H1 2026 show a stabilizing demand picture with emerging quality risks. Ticket volume peaked in March at 1,305 and has since declined steadily, falling 15% by June — consistent with the seasonal pattern described in the 2026 Nordic Service Desk Benchmark (see attached benchmark report). The demand outlook for Q3 is therefore manageable even at current staffing levels.

Resolution health is encouraging. Escalations are down 14% from their March peak, and with ticket volume falling in parallel, the data indicates that the resolution process is healthier than at the start of the year. The staffing gap noted by the Head of Support (two senior departures in March, replacements starting in August) appears to have been absorbed without material operational impact.

The main watch item is customer sentiment: CSAT has declined from 4.1 in January to 3.7 in June, a trend that merits attention in Q3 even though operational indicators remain solid.
